title: JIT skills — schema-shaped, macro-rendered, composable-on-demand guidance via contextBlocks; skill-blocks as single source projecting to SKILL.md + in-pi fragments
description: Vision (user 2026-05-25): skills join the JIT paradigm the rest of the system already runs on — agents JIT-compiled (pi-jit-agents), capabilities JIT-composed-from-empty (FEAT-005), context macro-rendered + injected (contextBlocks / render-item-by-id / templates macros), reads composed-to-need (FGAP-103). Today a skill is the ODD-ONE-OUT static markdown build-artifact (skill-narrative.md -> generate-skills -> SKILL.md). JIT skills = skill-blocks (schema-shaped substrate) rendered via Nunjucks macros, composed on demand scoped to need, delivered through the SAME contextBlocks channel as substrate data. SINGLE SOURCE / TWO PROJECTIONS: skill-blocks -> macro -> (a) SKILL.md for the external/marketplace+Claude-Code surface (replacing hand-written skill-narrative), (b) composable contextBlock fragments for in-pi JIT delivery. Unifies agents+skills as two instances of ONE JIT prompt-artifact compilation primitive in pi-jit-agents (DRY — the recurring 'don't repeat the code for different things'). REFRAMES FGAP-090 as ROUTE C, superseding its A/B fork (A inject-static-skills = always-on budget cost; B fold-into-descriptions = no home for cross-cutting guidance): JIT guidance is composed-when-relevant and reaches the agent without either cost. RELIEVES (does not kill) FGAP-085 — descriptions stay lean discovery+grounding; depth lives in composable skill-blocks. Intersects FGAP-103 (element-read of skill fragments — no read-all-or-nothing on skills), FGAP-101 (progressive), FGAP-090 (delivery solved). Generalizes the empty-state-config-driven philosophy (DEC-0015/0011/0025) from substrate/agents/capabilities to GUIDANCE itself. OPEN DESIGN FORK (UNSETTLED — must be decided before/at implementation; this FEAT carries the question, not the answer): WHO composes and WHEN. (a) COMPILER-COMPOSES — the dispatching context selects relevant skill-blocks and injects them at compile/dispatch time, declaratively (like agent contextBlocks: the agent spec / dispatch names the skills it needs). (b) AGENT-PULLS — the agent element-reads skill fragments on demand via tools as it works (like read-block element-level / FGAP-103). Likely HYBRID (baseline orientation compiler-composed + deep fragments agent-pulled), but the relevance-SELECTION mechanism is the crux and is unsettled: explicit contextBlocks declaration vs composition-lens-by-tag/relation vs agent-side retrieval. The fork determines the primitive's shape; settle before implementation. DELIVERY MECHANISM CONFIRMED (2026-05-25, pi 0.75.4): the JIT-skill in-pi delivery channel = pi's own extension hooks (exact adherence). resources_discover ({ skillPaths }) surfaces skill-block-rendered fragments as eager <available_skills> metadata (dist/core/extensions/types.d.ts:371/377/784; dist/core/skills.js:258-277 formatSkillsForPrompt); before_agent_start ({ systemPrompt }) injects the eager pointer/orientation block (types.d.ts:469/738/796; dist/core/agent-session.js:791-792; dist/core/extensions/runner.js:710-739 chaining); bodies fetched lazily via the element-read tools (FGAP-103). The OPEN compiler-composes-vs-agent-pulls fork maps directly onto these: compiler-composes = before_agent_start eager injection; agent-pulls = lazy element-read of contributed skill paths/blocks (likely hybrid). Single-source/two-projections still holds: skill-blocks -> macro -> SKILL.md (external) AND -> resources_discover/before_agent_start fragments (in-pi).
motivation: Skills are the last static, non-JIT surface in a system where everything else is schema-shaped data + macro-rendered + composed-from-empty-to-need. Folding skills into that paradigm: makes authored guidance actually REACH the in-pi agent (closes FGAP-090, which today gives the agent zero passive benefit from the whole skill-narrative investment); is DRY (one schema'd source, two projections, one compile primitive shared with agents); and makes guidance composable/scoped rather than a monolithic blob. It is the capstone of the JIT/constitutional arc applied to guidance.
acceptance_criteria: Skill content is schema-shaped (a skill block kind), not hand-authored freeform markdown
Skill-blocks render to prompt text via Nunjucks macros, reusing the existing macro system (no new bespoke renderer)
generate-skills projects skill-blocks -> SKILL.md (external/marketplace surface) from the schema'd source — replacing the hand-written skill-narrative pipeline

Skill fragments are delivered to the in-pi agent via the contextBlocks channel — verified in a live constrained-pi session that the composed guidance is in the agent's reported context (the FGAP-090 empirical test)
Skills are composable/reusable on demand, scoped to the task (not a monolithic SKILL.md blob)
Element-level read of skill fragments exists (no read-all-or-nothing on skills; routes through the FGAP-103 shared primitive)
The compose model (compiler-composes vs agent-pulls vs hybrid) AND the relevance-selection mechanism are DECIDED and implemented (the open fork resolved)
Agents and skills share ONE JIT prompt-artifact compilation primitive in pi-jit-agents (not two codepaths)
